summary: The desk is overtrading at 9/4 weekly buys into a book where 3 of 5 holdings sit under live/standing dilution or insider-distribution overhangs (UUUU $300M ATM + 7/9 insider Form 144, NTSK post-lockup VC distribution, WRAP $200M baby-shelf), while every scan candidate above the pack (JEM/PRQR/TDIC) is a verified dilution/pump trap already vetoed -- no NEW offering hit EDGAR today; all 5 positions reconcile to the broker and the CAI cache/broker drift has converged.

WRAP.note: $200M universal S-3 baby-shelf on file (last S-3 activity Feb 2026) on a sub-$100M post-+48%-pump name = standing drip-dilution capacity; EDGAR shows NO July 424B5 draw, 7/13 8-K is a WrapShield product launch not a raise. Radar 10.73 (held). Position +$1.37 at $2.12. FinBERT -0.853 'unusual volume' item is noise.

UUUU.note: Active $300M ATM (equity distribution agreement 2026-06-13) is a live dilution mechanism; EDGAR verified NO July draw (7/14 8-K is an investor presentation). Form 144 (Chalmers, 7/9) is a proposed insider SALE so the insider picture behind the score (Bhappu/Hansen buys) is now mixed. Lorentzian k-NN -0.75 bearish; position -$1.32 at $13.25.

CAI.note: Fundamentals clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect launch, $100M buyback, no dilution/shelf). Downgraded from elevated: this morning's audit broker_disagrees drift (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99) has CONVERGED intraday -- broker now $16.95, cache $16.99, reconciliation status 'match'. Position -$0.77.

NTSK.note: Book's best gainer +$5.91 at $14.22, breakout catalyst (Wells Fargo/Jefferies/Cantor Buy) intact, but post-IPO-lockup VC distribution is ongoing: EDGAR Form 144 for 1.75M Class A sh listed for sale (7/8) atop the 7/9 Lightspeed XII sale; Iconiq's 7/8 buy only partly offsets. The +7.2% pop is masking net distribution.

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing; failed Feb-2026 take-private replaced by an anti-dilutive $140M self-tender buyback. Position +$0.80 at $5.49, no fresh negative catalyst.

flags.why: Top non-held scan name (5.2, below line) but a 12-for-1 reverse split was effective TODAY (7/14, new CUSIP G8071C137) purely for Nasdaq $1 bid-price compliance (Rule 5550(a)(2)), shares cut 8.06M->~672k, on an unfunded 'AI+blockchain+crypto/stablecoin' pivot pump (+130/-39/+27 whipsaw). Reverse-split-plus-crypto-pump, not a fundamental catalyst. FinBERT +0.919 is a misattributed generic-market headline.

Scan candidate (5.17) but management just diluted ~30%: EDGAR-verified $50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement priced 6/25, closed 6/26, plus a 7/6 F-3 registering 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. Stock ~$1.72 sits BELOW the $1.81 offer; the CEO's $1.59 7/7 buy that drives the insider score came AFTER the raise.

Scan candidate (5.1) with a textbook pump-and-dump signature: HK event-management microcap ran ~$0.23 (6/12) to >$10 intraday (6/16) then collapsed to ~$3.57. Extreme two-way volatility, no fundamental driver; reject on the pump veto. Cache also stale per nightly audit.

Scan candidate (2.96, below line) with a severe dilution overhang: ~167M shares out, active $300M S-3 shelf, 13.95M warrants, authorized shares hiked to 450M. Phase 2 news constructive but capital structure unresolved; desk avoid.

Distressed reverse-merger pop, not organic strength: definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Still on today's scan (2.06) with a -3.0 internal risk penalty.

On the daily watchlist (framed on the Equinor $1B+ backlog) but a pending ~$5.8B all-stock acquisition of Valaris (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh) dilutes existing holders to ~53% of the combined company and now carries a DOJ 'second request' -- parties won't certify before 7/31, pushing earliest close to ~9/29. Material dilution + binary-vote risk.

Watchlist name that priced a $100M underwritten offering (13,495,277 sh at $7.41) on 6/24 -- real dilution alongside the ABS-201 Phase 1 catalyst; ~$10 now sits above mean analyst PT. Keep flagged so it is not bought as a fresh breakout.

Most recent 10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant overhang). FinBERT -0.288 on the cash-runway story. Not a current scan candidate but keep hard-vetoed if it resurfaces.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 2026-01-13, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On the research avoid list; keep vetoed.
Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. Desk avoid list; not held.

Distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- distress signal, not a turnaround.

dilution_watch.note: $300M ATM equity distribution agreement, usable now. EDGAR verified NO July draw (7/14 8-K is investor presentation only); Form 144 (Chalmers, 7/9) is a proposed insider sale.
$200M universal baby-shelf on file for a sub-$100M post-pump name; EDGAR verified NO July 424B5 draw -- standing dilution capacity only.
$50M underwritten registered direct (27,624,310 sh at $1.81) + $9.2M Lilly private placement (5,100,780 sh); closed 6/26. 7/6 F-3 registers 3,030,303 PIPE sh for resale. No NEW July offering.
12-for-1 reverse split effective today for Nasdaq $1 bid-price compliance -- corporate action that desyncs ledger vs broker; not a fundamental catalyst. Not held, so no ledger impact for this book.
~$5.8B all-stock Valaris acquisition (15.235 RIG sh per Valaris sh); existing holders diluted to ~53% of combined co; DOJ second request pushes earliest close to ~9/29.
$100M underwritten offering, 13,495,277 sh at $7.41; immediate dilution.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger + $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants; authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.

disagreements: Discipline (carried): radar 'Will it buy next scan?' shows weekly buy budget 9/4 used -- the book is well over the 4-buy weekly cap. Surface as a standalone overtrading flag independent of any single ticker.

RIG: intel's 7/14 alert now concurs on the ~$5.8B Valaris all-stock deal + DOJ second request and tags it 'do-not-chase' (progress), but still carries RIG on the active watchlist; I keep it an elevated DO-NOT-BUY given ~53% dilution and a binary shareholder-vote/antitrust event.

AVO (Mission Produce): now the top non-held scan candidate (5.24) on a +5.0 insider score, but EDGAR shows that 'insider' signal is GlobalHarvest/Jay Pack strategic accumulation that triggered a poison-pill rights plan -- a takeover-accumulation dynamic, not management conviction. No S-3/ATM; only 2026 dilution was the completed Calavo acquisition (S-4/424B3, closed 5/28), offset by a $100M buyback. Not a dilution/pump veto and below the buy line, so no flag -- but the score's insider component is mis-attributed.
Convergence (not a conflict): intel's flags_for_bot now lists JEM, TDIC, PRQR and WRAP, matching this snapshot; the earlier PRQR/WRAP/NTSK-distribution disagreements are resolved.
